mission.title: argo-events: Infinite creation of new Workflows.
mission.description: Infinite creation of new Workflows.. This issue affects 7+ users.

steps.title: Identify argo-events troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your argo-events deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n argo-events -l app.kubernetes.io/name=argo-events
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=argo-events -n argo-events --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review argo-events configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant argo-events configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n argo-events -l app.kubernetes.io/name=argo-events
kubectl get configmap -n argo-events -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=argo-events
```
When we start Argo Workflows it works ok, but in some moment (let's say two or three hours later) it starts creating new workflows and it never stops.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Infinite creation of new Workflows.
steps.description:
When connection/channel is closed, it keeps reading empty messages from the channel without checking if it's successful.
```yaml
apiVersion: argoproj.io/v1alpha1
kind: Sensor
metadata:
  name: amqp
  namespace: argo-events
spec:
  template:
    serviceAccountName: events
  dependencies:
    - name: test-dep
      eventSourceName: amqp
      eventName: example
  triggers:
    - template:
        name: amqp-workflow-trigger
        k8s:
          group: argoproj.io
          version: v1alpha1
          resource: workflows
          operation: create
          source:
            resource:
              apiVersion: argoproj.io/v1alpha1
              kind: Workflow
              metadata:
                namespace: argo-wor
```

steps.title: Confirm Infinite creation of new Workflows. is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=argo-events -n argo-events --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n argo-events --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary: When connection/channel is closed, it keeps reading empty messages from the channel without checking if it's successful.
codeSnippets:
apiVersion: argoproj.io/v1alpha1
kind: Sensor
metadata:
  name: amqp
  namespace: argo-events
spec:
  template:
    serviceAccountName: events
  dependencies:
    - name: test-dep
      eventSourceName: amqp
      eventName: example
  triggers:
    - template:
        name: amqp-workflow-trigger
        k8s:
          group: argoproj.io
          version: v1alpha1
          resource: workflows
          operation: create
          source:
            resource:
              apiVersion: argoproj.io/v1alpha1
              kind: Workflow
              metadata:
                namespace: argo-workflows
                generateName: sync-by-id-
              spec:
                entrypoint: sync
                arguments:
                  parameters:
                    - name: id

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with argo-events installed or the issue environment reproducible.
